action_errors.definition: { error: 'NOT_FOUND' | 'NOT_VISIBLE' | 'DISABLED' | 'BUSY' | 'OPTION_NOT_FOUND'; id: string; reason?: string; available?: string[] }
action_errors.throw_mechanism: Thrown as plain objects (not Error instances) using TypeScript satisfies pattern: 'throw { error: "NOT_FOUND", id } satisfies ProbeActionError'
action_errors.where_thrown: sdk/web/src/actions/dispatcher.ts — preCheck() private method + select() method
pre_check_execution_order: 1. NOT_FOUND — element must be in registry
4. BUSY — state.current must not be 'loading' or 'submitting'
5. OPTION_NOT_FOUND — (select() only) value must be in data.options if options are declared

errors.description: The element with the given probe ID is not registered in the element registry.

fields.id: the probe ID that was not found

errors.code_location: sdk/web/src/actions/dispatcher.ts line 43: 'if (!el) throw { error: "NOT_FOUND", id } satisfies ProbeActionError' and line 367: 'if (!element) throw { error: "NOT_FOUND", id }'
errors.resolution: Verify the element has data-probe-id annotation matching the ID used in the test. Run probe.query(id) to confirm. Check that the element is in the DOM when the action is attempted.

errors.description: The element is registered but its layout.visible flag is false — it is hidden from the viewport.

fields.id: the probe ID of the hidden element

errors.resolution: Use isEffectivelyVisible(id) to check the full ancestor chain. If a parent container is hidden, the element will be NOT_VISIBLE even if its own visible flag were true. Use waitForState() to wait for the containing element to become visible first.
errors.note: layout.visible is set by the LayoutTracker via IntersectionObserver (web) or equivalent platform visibility APIs. This is geometric visibility, not CSS display or opacity.

errors.description: The element is in 'disabled' state and cannot accept interactions.

fields.id: the probe ID of the disabled element
fields.reason: optional — validation error messages if state.validationErrors are set (joined as 'field: message; ...')

errors.code_location: sdk/web/src/actions/dispatcher.ts lines 373-377: 'if (element.state.current === "disabled") { const reason = element.state.validationErrors?.map(e => `${e.field}: ${e.message}`).join("; "); throw { error: "DISABLED", id, reason }; }'
errors.resolution: Check what condition drives the disabled state. If ENABLED_TOGGLE linkage is involved, a prerequisite element may need to be interacted with first. Use getState(id) to inspect the current state and validationErrors.

errors.description: The element is in 'loading' or 'submitting' state and is not ready for interaction.

fields.id: the probe ID of the busy element

errors.code_location: sdk/web/src/actions/dispatcher.ts lines 379-381: 'if (element.state.current === "loading" || element.state.current === "submitting") throw { error: "BUSY", id, reason: `Element is ${element.state.current}` }'
errors.resolution: Call waitForState(id, 'loaded') or waitForPageReady() before the action to ensure the element is ready. For form submission, wait for state to leave 'submitting' before re-interacting.

errors.description: The select() action was called with a value that is not in the element's declared options list.
errors.trigger_condition: element.data?.options is set AND value is not in element.data.options

fields.id: the probe ID of the selector element
fields.available: element.data.options — array of valid option strings

errors.code_location: sdk/web/src/actions/dispatcher.ts lines 122-128: 'if (element.data?.options && !element.data.options.includes(value)) throw { error: "OPTION_NOT_FOUND", id, available: element.data.options }'
errors.note: Only thrown when data.options is explicitly declared. If no options are declared, the select proceeds without this check.
errors.resolution: Check the available[] array in the error object for valid options. If options are dynamic (loaded from API), call waitForSource(id) before select() to ensure options are populated.

timeout_errors.description: Timeout errors are thrown as Error instances (not ProbeActionError) when promise-based wait operations exceed their timeout.

errors.code_location: sdk/web/src/collector/state-observer.ts line 97: 'reject(new Error(`waitForState(\'${id}\', \'${state}\') timed out after ${timeout}ms`))'
errors.resolution: Check that the element's state annotation is being updated by the application. Verify the state string matches exactly (case-sensitive). Increase timeout if network is slow.

errors.message_format: waitForPageReady timed out. Still loading: {comma-separated element IDs}
errors.code_location: sdk/web/src/probe.ts lines 297-298 and 319: includes the IDs of still-loading elements
errors.resolution: The error message includes which elements are still loading. Investigate why those elements haven't reached a terminal state. Verify the PAGE element or individual elements update their data-probe-state attribute.

errors.error_type: Captured as timeout result in LinkageResult (not thrown)
errors.code_location: sdk/web/src/actions/dispatcher.ts line 277: 'result: "timeout"' in the catch block
errors.resolution: A timeout result means the linked target element did not emit a state-change event within 5 seconds. Verify the data flow is actually wired up and the target element has a probe annotation.

other_errors.code_location: sdk/web/src/probe.ts line 267: 'if (!el) throw new Error(`Element not found: ${id}`)'
other_errors.note: Unlike action methods which throw ProbeActionError, getState throws a plain Error. This is an inconsistency between State Exposure and Action Dispatch error handling.

pre_check_pattern.description: The pre-check pattern is the core safety mechanism of Action Dispatch. It is implemented as a private preCheck() method that returns the validated ProbeElement for further use.
pre_check_pattern.pattern_code: private preCheck(id: string): ProbeElement { const element = this.registry.query(id); if (!element) throw { error: 'NOT_FOUND', id }; if (!element.layout.visible) throw { error: 'NOT_VISIBLE', id }; if (element.state.current === 'disabled') throw { error: 'DISABLED', id, reason }; if (element.state.current === 'loading' || element.state.current === 'submitting') throw { error: 'BUSY', id, reason }; return element; }
pre_check_pattern.usage: Every action method except pressShortcut() and navigate() calls preCheck(id) as its first statement. select() uses the returned element to check options.
exceptions_to_pattern: pressShortcut() — dispatched to document, not an element; no element pre-checks
navigate() — operates on window.history, not an element; no element pre-checks
drag() — calls preCheck() on BOTH sourceId and targetId before proceeding

custom_matchers.description: The Playwright integration provides 3 custom expect matchers that build on top of the error model.

matchers.name: toHaveProbeState
matchers.behavior: Retries probe.getState() in a timeout loop (not event-driven). Passes when state.current matches. Fails with message showing actual vs expected state.
matchers.timeout: configurable, default from Playwright expect timeout

matchers.name: toHaveProbeData
matchers.behavior: Deep partial match of the element's data field. Passes if all provided fields match (extra fields in actual are ignored).

matchers.name: toBeEffectivelyVisible
matchers.behavior: Calls isEffectivelyVisible(id) which walks the ancestor chain. Fails if any ancestor has visible=false.
